exercises.name: Joint Circles Warm-Up
exercises.instructions: 30 sec each: wrist circles, elbow circles, shoulder circles forward and backward. 30 sec: hip circles. 30 sec: ankle circles. 10x neck semi-circle (ear to shoulder, chin to chest, other side — NO full neck circles backward). 10x thoracic rotation each side.

exercises.name: Incline Push-Up
exercises.instructions: Hands on bench, table edge, or windowsill. Lower chest toward surface, elbows 45° from body (not flared wide). Push back up to straight arms. Keep body straight as a plank — no sagging hips. Progress to flat floor push-ups when 12 reps feel easy.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press — Seated
exercises.instructions: Sit upright on chair, feet flat, spine neutral. Hold dumbbells at shoulder height, palms forward, elbows at 90°. Press straight up until arms extended (don't lock). Lower slowly — 3 seconds down.
cues: Do NOT arch lumbar to press heavier — weight is too heavy if you do

exercises.name: Lateral Raise
exercises.instructions: Standing or seated, dumbbells at sides, slight bend at elbow. Raise dumbbells out to the side until arms are horizontal — no higher. Lower slowly (3 sec).
cues: Thumbs slightly down (like pouring a glass) — protects rotator cuff

exercises.name: Tricep Overhead Extension
exercises.instructions: Seated. Hold one dumbbell with both hands overhead, arms extended. Bend at elbows only — lower dumbbell behind head. Extend back up.
cues: Upper arms close to ears, pointing straight up

exercises.name: Stick Chest Pass
exercises.instructions: Stand, hold stick horizontally with both hands, arms extended at chest height. Slowly push the stick forward (extending elbows), then pull back. Variation: push stick up at 45° then return.
cues: Feel the thoracic activation on the pull-back

exercises.name: Dumbbell Floor Press
exercises.instructions: Lie flat on back, knees bent. Hold dumbbell in each hand at lower chest level, elbows at ~45° (NOT flared wide). Press both up until arms fully extended. Pause 1 second. Lower slowly — 3 seconds. The floor prevents shoulder hyperextension.
cues: Floor is your safety limit for shoulder depth

exercises.name: Scapular Push-Up Isolation
exercises.instructions: Standard push-up position (arms extended). Keep elbows LOCKED straight. Push floor away — protract (push apart) shoulder blades, rounding upper back slightly. Hold 3 seconds. Allow blades to come gently together. Repeat. This is a tiny, precise movement.
cues: Elbows locked straight the entire time

exercises.name: Archer Push-Up
exercises.instructions: Start in a wide push-up position. As you lower, shift weight to one side — that arm bends while the opposite arm stays nearly straight (like an archer drawing a bow). Alternate or complete all reps on one side then switch.
cues: Shift weight toward the bending arm

exercises.name: Slow-Tempo / Diamond Push-Up
exercises.instructions: Standard push-up or diamond (index fingers and thumbs touching) for tricep emphasis. MANDATORY tempo: 3 seconds down, 1 second hold at bottom, 1 second up. No bouncing out of the bottom.

exercises.name: Single-Arm Floor Press
exercises.instructions: Same as floor press. Press one dumbbell at a time. The non-pressing arm rests by your side. One-arm pressing requires core and obliques to resist rotation — doubles as anti-rotation core work.
cues: Non-pressing arm rests at side — don't counter-balance

exercises.name: One-Arm Dumbbell Row
exercises.instructions: Put left knee and left hand on a chair for support. Hold dumbbell in right hand, arm hanging down, spine neutral and horizontal. Pull dumbbell up toward hip, leading with elbow, squeezing the back. Lower slowly.
cues: Spine FLAT like a table — not rounded
Pull to HIP, not toward shoulder
exercises.warning: Back must remain flat. This targets rhomboids and middle trapezius — critical anti-kyphosis muscles.

exercises.name: Bicep Curl
exercises.instructions: Stand upright, dumbbells at sides, palms facing forward. Curl both (or alternating) toward shoulders. Lower slowly — 3 seconds down.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Fly
exercises.instructions: Sit on edge of chair, lean forward so chest nearly touches thighs (spine neutral, not rounded). Dumbbells hanging below, palms facing each other. With slight elbow bend, raise arms out to sides until horizontal. Squeeze shoulder blades. Lower slowly.
cues: Spine neutral when leaned forward — not rounded
2-3 kg only — this directly targets kyphosis muscles

exercises.name: Face Pull with Stick
exercises.instructions: Hold stick with both hands, elbows bent, palms facing you. Pull stick toward your face while flaring elbows out and squeezing shoulder blades together hard at end range. Extend slowly. Alternatively use resistance band at head height.
cues: Elbows flare OUT and UP on the pull
Maximum shoulder blade squeeze at end range
Arguably the most important postural exercise

exercises.name: Stick Pull-Apart
exercises.instructions: Hold stick with both hands, shoulder-width grip, arms extended in front at chest height. Keeping arms straight, pull the stick apart (as if trying to break it) while moving arms out to sides until stick touches chest. Return slowly.
cues: Neutral spine — don't extend lower back
Opens chest and activates rhomboids directly

exercises.name: Hammer Curl
exercises.instructions: Same as bicep curl but palms face each other (neutral grip, like holding a hammer) throughout the entire movement.
cues: Neutral grip (thumbs up) the entire time

exercises.name: Shoulder External Rotation — Side-Lying
exercises.instructions: Lie on left side, left arm supporting head. Hold VERY light dumbbell (1-2 kg) in right hand. Bend right elbow to 90°, upper arm resting against your side, forearm lying across waist. Keeping upper arm locked against ribs, rotate forearm UPWARD until vertical (or as high as comfortable). Lower slowly — 3 seconds.
cues: Upper arm PINNED against torso — must not move
This is corrective, not strength — load is intentionally minimal
exercises.warning: If you feel it in shoulder blade instead of back of shoulder, reduce weight. Rotator cuff corrective — universally weak in kyphosis.

exercises.name: Prone I-Y-T Raises
exercises.instructions: Lie face down on mat. I position: arms straight overhead. Y position: narrow V. T position: arms horizontal at 90°. For each position, gently lift arms 5-8 cm. Hold 3 sec. Lower. 1 set per position before moving exercises.

exercises.name: Inverted Row — Table Edge
exercises.instructions: Find a sturdy table. Lie under it on your back. Reach up and grip the far edge with both hands, slightly wider than shoulder width, palms facing you. Keep body completely straight. Pull chest up to the table bottom — elbows pulling back, shoulder blades squeezing together. Lower slowly — 3 sec.
cues: Body stays straight — like a reverse plank
The more horizontal you are, the harder it is
Begin at a steep angle and progress to horizontal
exercises.warning: Ensure table is very sturdy before performing.

exercises.name: Inverted Row — Feet Elevated
exercises.instructions: Same as table-edge inverted row, but now elevate feet on a chair so body is fully horizontal. This dramatically increases the percentage of bodyweight you are lifting.
cues: Squeeze shoulder blades hard at top
Full range: chest touches table edge
exercises.warning: Ensure both table and chair are very stable.

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
exercises.instructions: Hold one dumbbell vertically, cupped between both hands at chest height. Feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out. Descend slowly — chest up, heels on floor, knees tracking over toes. Descend to just parallel or as deep as comfortable. Drive up through heels.
cues: Dumbbell at chest counterbalances and keeps spine upright
Knees track over toes — don't cave inward

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift — Light
exercises.instructions: Stand, dumbbells in front of thighs. With slight knee bend and neutral spine, hinge at hips — push hips back, lower dumbbells along your shins. Stop when you feel hamstring stretch (roughly shin level). Drive hips forward to return.
cues: Spine NEUTRAL throughout — natural curves maintained
Feel the hinge in the hips, not a bend in the back
Hips push BACK — not a squat down
exercises.warning: If thoracic spine rounds, reduce range of motion. Spine must stay neutral.

exercises.name: Reverse Lunge
exercises.instructions: Stand with dumbbells at sides. Step one foot BACKWARD and lower back knee toward (not touching) the floor. Front knee stays above ankle, torso upright. Push through front heel to return.
cues: Step back far enough so front shin stays vertical
Push through front heel to return

exercises.name: Stick-Assisted Sumo Squat
exercises.instructions: Hold stick vertically in front of you for balance. Feet wider than shoulders, toes turned out 45°. Squat down slowly, keeping stick vertical and back straight.

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.instructions: Forearm plank: elbows under shoulders, body straight from head to heels. Hold 20 seconds, build to 45 seconds over weeks.
cues: Hips do NOT droop or pike up
Breathe NORMALLY — never hold breath in plank

exercises.name: Pallof Press
exercises.instructions: Attach resistance band to door handle at waist height. Stand sideways ~1 metre from anchor, feet shoulder-width. Hold band with both hands at chest. Press hands straight out — resist the rotational pull with your core. Hold extended 3-5 sec. Return.
cues: You do NOT rotate — you PREVENT rotation
Hold extended position 3-5 sec before returning

exercises.name: Glute Bridge — Loaded
exercises.instructions: Lie on back, dumbbell resting on lower abdomen/hip crease. Drive through heels to lift hips. Squeeze glutes hard at top. Lower slowly — 3 sec.

exercises.name: Walking Lunge — Stick Overhead
exercises.instructions: Hold gym stick horizontally above head with both hands, arms fully extended. Step forward into a lunge, lowering back knee toward floor. Keep stick overhead and torso upright. Push up through front heel to standing, then advance alternating legs across the room.
cues: Torso stays upright — if you lean forward the stick reveals it
Alternative: stick across shoulders if overhead not yet possible

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.instructions: Stand in split stance in front of a chair. Place rear foot on top of chair behind you. Lower back knee straight down toward floor — 3 second descent. Push back up through front heel. Hold dumbbells at sides.
cues: Front knee stays above ankle — not forward of toes
Front knee does not cave inward

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift — Heavier
exercises.instructions: Stand with dumbbell in each hand, palms facing you. Hinge at hips — push them back lowering dumbbells along shins while keeping back FLAT. Lower until hamstring stretch (mid-shin level). Drive hips forward to standing, squeeze glutes at top.
cues: Hip HINGE — not a back-rounding exercise
Reduce to knee-level range if lower back rounds at all
Squeeze glutes fully at the top
exercises.warning: Do not perform until 3×15 glute bridge with dumbbell completed in Phase 2. If lower back rounds AT ANY POINT, reduce range of motion. Scheuermann's means perfectly flat back is not always achievable — reduce range rather than force.
